description: Video. It is a static informational message driven entirely by speech, with no music track and no fast cutting. | Hello, I am Alejandro Mayorkas, Secretary of the United States Department of Homeland Security. The Department of Homeland Security has a critical role in the fight against human trafficking. This work touches nearly every part of our department. Our extraordinary special agents identify, save, and protect victims, and they investigate and arrest the traffickers. We deliver support to survivors of human trafficking to ensure their safety and stability. We provide immigration relief to non-citizen trafficking victims and their families. We ensure that goods produced by forced labor do not come into our country. We train law enforcement on how to recognize indicators of human trafficking. description: Video in which HSI's Robert Lombardi speaks to camera in front of a Cyber Crimes Center (C3) backdrop, telling parents that online predators target children over the holidays and directing viewers to ice.gov/sextortion. It is a single speaker with captions and no music track. | I'm Robert Lombardi with Homeland Security Investigations, Child Exploitation and Investigations Unit. I want to remind parents that online predators are praying on our unsuspective children more than ever. Despite our guidance to kids not to talk to strangers, our kids are often doing exactly that right under our noses, in our living rooms, in the back seats of our cars, in the classrooms and more using mobile devices. Never before have our children been so vulnerable. And this is especially a concern over the holidays when our kids have more time on their hands. And criminals know it. For example, sex torsion, the act of threatening to share nude or explicit images for money is more a concern than you may think. And the current trend targets are 14 to 70-year-old children. tweet_text: Tis the season for shopping and staying safe online. Scammers look to your personal and financial info to make their season bright. Protect yourself and your family before shopping online with these tips from the team at @CISAgov. ⬇️ #12DaysofTips https://t.co/TAqq4gUnwz
description: A holiday cyber-safety video in which a CISA spokesperson wearing a Christmas sweater speaks to camera in front of a CISA step-and-repeat backdrop, advising viewers to use multi-factor authentication, shop trusted sources, and avoid phishing, ending at cisa.gov/shopsafely. It is a single speaker in a static setting, consisting of speech with no music track. | Happy Holidays! Sissa wants everyone to enjoy the holiday season, but we know that while you're breaking out your ugly sweater, bad actors are trying to steal your joy, your identity, and your money. How can you stop these scrooches in their tracks? First, always ensure you have multi-factor authentication turned on. Passwords can be hacked, so add one more level of security, your fingerprint, your face, something only you have. Second, shop through trusted sources, and watch out for fishing emails. Never give your account info out via text for email. Finally, if your password is still 12345, you're getting a hole in your stock. Learn more at sissa.gov slash shop safely. Happy Holidays! | Reviewed video keyframe (direct frame analysis); media form recorded via the candidate tags above.

tweet_text: This National Apprenticeship Week, @USDOL is proud to highlight stories from apprentices across the country. Thanks to Registered Apprenticeship programs at @ETI569, apprentices like Matthew are building in-demand skills and on a pathway to good-paying, high-quality careers. https://t.co/gEZcpRCc2z
description: A talking-head video for National Apprenticeship Week in which Matthew, an electrical apprentice, stands before an Electrical Training Institute (ETI) banner flanked by flags and describes his five-year program. It is a subtitled interview with B-roll and no music track. | My name is Matthew Boteca. I am currently in apprentice at ETI. I am currently in my third year and it is a five-year-long program. At the end of the five years you will get your journeyman license, become a licensed electrician in the state of California and plenty of opportunities will await you at that point. Currently I'm working in the solar field, ranging from the underground aspect of it going up to the actual panels. description: My name's Jennifer Boss. It's my daughter Megan, and I came here to tell you about her. She was my first born child. She was my only daughter. She was a mother, a beloved sister, an aunt of fiercely loyal friend. Megan had been planning to return to college and start her own business. She was really bubbly and creative, and she always did the craziest things to make somebody laugh when they were starting to fill down. That's who she was. The mischievous sparkle in her eye, you can see it. The love that she generously gave to everybody. And the hope that she had for her future was erased by someone who had no right to be in this country. She went to his house, and she never left. He had taken her body and stuffed it in a garbage can, keeping her doused in bleach for 51 days while we searched for her desperately. That's why we fight for her every day. This has been incredibly hard on our family, especially right now. Tomorrow marks one year since they found her. A tragedy like mine and the other families that are here, excuse me, could have been prevented. And that knowledge creates an agony for us like no other. description: Here in the shadows of monuments to George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, honoring the British King, might seem an ironic beginning to our celebration of 250 years of American independence. But in fact, no tribute could be more appropriate. Long before Americans had a nation or a constitution, we first had a culture, a character, and a creed. Before we ever proclaimed our independence, Americans carried within us the rarest of gifts, moral courage, and it came from a small but mighty kingdom from across the sea. For nearly two centuries before the revolution this land was settled and forged by men, women who bore in their souls the blood and noble spirit of the British here on a wild and untamed continent. They set loose the ancient English love of liberty and the great Britain's distinctive sense of glory, destiny, and pride, and that's what it is, glory, destiny, and pride. The American patriots who pledged their lives to independence in 1776 were the heirs to this majestic inheritance. tweet_text: Under the Trump administration, we have effectively ended “catch and release." Thanks to our actions, illegal aliens will no longer be released into our country. https://t.co/47HHfTXVky

.@DHS_Wolf: “Today, @ICEgov no longer exempts any removable aliens from having our laws enforced on them. If you break our laws, you will be arrested and removed from our country." https://t.co/pq7wEec3Ro
.@USBPChief explains how the new border wall system has made border communities safer in both the United States AND Mexico. https://t.co/JF54v0zIks

.@DHS_Wolf explains the effectiveness of the new border wall system: “In the @CBPRGV Sector, our @CBP agents observe that human smugglers are changing their tactics due to the new border wall system and are attempting to cross through areas that are easier to surveil.” https://t.co/3vMoChN5Tf

.@DHS_wolf warns about the dangerous polices calling to stop construction and dismantle the effective border wall system. "Let me be crystal clear: each of these measures would endanger the lives of the Border Patrol and Americans across this country." https://t.co/p7RzJBQuQT

.@DHS_Wolf responds to critics: "The many miles of border wall system exists because of the will and the vision of @POTUS, and the dedication and hard work of the men and women of @CBP and @usacehq." https://t.co/cL0NBmb77J

We have been working around the clock to reform our immigration system, strengthen border security, & enforce the rule of law. @HomelandKen: ”The broken system & detrimental policies that were inherited at that time from the previous admin were really a complete disaster.” https://t.co/4fjLgrtqGq
"Our top priority at @DHSgov and @CISAgov is to ensure American voters decide American elections." @DHS_Wolf provides an update on #ElectionSecurity #Protect2020 https://t.co/dFbp4zf9ww

"Let me be clear: Our election infrastructure is resilient, and we have no indications that a foreign actor has succeeded in compromising or affecting the actual votes cast in this election." - @DHS_Wolf on #ElectionSecurity #Protect2020 https://t.co/9gCg4YO8Xa

.@DHS_Wolf lays out what the federal govt has done to secure #ElectionDay: "We began laying the groundwork to secure this year’s election long ago by engaging in an unprecedented level of effort across the U.S. government with our state, local, and private sector partners." https://t.co/812JdcbO3J

"Rest assured however our partners at the state & local level are working around the clock to make sure each vote is counted properly, but it's important to recognize this process may require time." @DHS_Wolf explains the importance of patience in waiting for election results. https://t.co/Ep86zUqUuh

"Voting is a sacred right afforded to every American and we at DHS are proud to defend that right from any foreign influence that would seek to compromise it," says @DHS_Wolf. This #ElectionDay, DHS is committed to ensuring that only American voters decide American elections. https://t.co/KHBx43Haa5

description: A local TV news segment ('DIGGING DEEPER / FRAUD IN MINNESOTA / TARGETED REVIEW') with an anchor reporting on a Department of Labor review of alleged unemployment-insurance fraud in Minnesota, including a full-screen quote card from Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer. It is a news broadcast. | Digging deeper tonight into being termed a targeted review of potential fraud in Minnesota's unemployment insurance program. It's the latest federal probe into allegations of financial wrongdoing this one by the U.S. Department of Labor. The Secretary of Labor, writing in a letter sent to the Minnesota Department of Employment and Economic Development, quote, I am appalled at what we are hearing about potential fraud coming from numerous benefits programs in Minnesota. If there has been any related abuse of our U.I. systems, it will not be tolerated. End quote. U.I., by the way, is a reference to unemployment insurance. The Labor Secretary says the Department's Chicago Regional Office has started a review of its benefit payment control operations to ensure no fraud is identified in unemployment insurance programs, including those during the COVID-19 pandemic.

tweet_text: Our ICE officers and agents are HEROES. On March 25, 2026, an ICE agent helping to support @TSA operations at John F. Kennedy International Airport in New York jumped into action to help save the life of a one-year-old child experiencing a medical emergency. The heroic actions of this officer began when a one-year-old child became unresponsive in the arms of his father, unable to breathe for almost two minutes. CCTV review shows a passenger in a TSA Precheck line holding his one-year-old in his arms when the child’s arms go lifeless; panic ensues, and the father is seen scrambling around the area, and calling for help. The agent working at his post, heard the screams from the father and other passengers and sprinted to the scene. The father handed the child to the officer, who then assessed the unresponsive child and began performing the Heimlich maneuver. After a few seconds, the child started breathing again. EMS personnel arrived on scene with medical equipment to further monitor and re-assess the child. The child was re-assessed and determined to be healthy enough to fly.

Johnstone faces multiple charges connected to animal abuse he allegedly committed while working at a local rescue in Nevada. The same day Johnstone was arrested, ICE issued an immigration detainer with the Clark County Detention Center. The next day, April 2, Clark County officials honored the detainer, and ICE took custody of the alleged animal abuser. Johnstone came to the United States under the Visa Waiver Program as a visitor. He was supposed to leave the country by Feb. 22, 2022. ICE secured a final order of removal. He’ll stay in ICE detention pending local charges.

description: Video. A TV 'Exit Interview' clip of Secretary Mayorkas (talking head with a subtitle bar) discussing drone sightings over New Jersey and asking Congress to expand DHS authorities. It is a broadcast interview, with no music. | And let me restate what we've said previously. It's very important. We know of no threat to people's security. And if we learn of any cause for concern, we will take action and we will communicate immediately with the American public. There are, and there have been thousands and thousands of drones flown across the United States for years. All drones, recreational drones, law enforcement drones. I spoke with an expert yesterday who has recorded over his home in California over the past six years, more than 13,000 drone detections. And so this is actually not a new phenomenon. What we need in the federal government is we need our authorities to catch up with the development of drone activity over the last 10 years. And so we have asked Congress for not only an extension of our authorities, but an expansion of them. | Reviewed video keyframe (direct frame analysis); media form recorded via the candidate tags above.

tweet_text: DETAINER ISSUED FOR ALLEGED NC MURDERER Venezuelan national Angelvis Jesus Quintero Fernandez was arrested for allegedly shooting a man to death in Mecklenburg County, NC, Jan. 24. ICE issued a detainer on him with the Mecklenburg County Jail March 23 to prevent him from reentering the community.
description: Horizontal video. A local TV news package about a Venezuelan national charged in a Mecklenburg County, NC murder. It includes a stand-up reporter, the suspect's mugshot, and on-screen fact bullets. It is a broadcast news clip reposted by ICE, with no music track. | We just learned Fernandez is being held without bond and has a court-appointed public defender. Fernandez is facing first-degree murder charges and it is related to the deadly shooting of 44-year-old Ludvin Guzman Morales along Central Avenue on January 24. Court records show that Fernandez was arrested yesterday and investigators say a break in the case came just days ago when officers responded to an attempted murder on Albumor Road. During the investigation, police say they found a gun inside Fernandez's car. They say he later admitted to shooting the victim after examining that weapon detectives believe it may be linked to the shooting of Guzman Morales. Now Fernandez also has an ice detainer meaning he could be taken into federal custody here at the Mecklenburg County Court House Fernandez is expected to be seen again on April 14th.
